design_philosophy.core_loop: Run through level stages, defeat enemies with projectile and melee attacks, reach boss encounter, progress to next level
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing limited ammunition pickups while maintaining forward momentum against wave enemies and platforming hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Batman in co-op with Robin, using gadgets and combat skills to stop Mr. Freeze's villainous scheme across Gotham
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Genesis version embraces run-and-gun action over exploration; emphasizes co-op teamplay as core feature distinct from SNES platformer variant; prioritizes continuous action pacing with instant respawns over careful pattern-learning

difficulty.lives_system: Batman starts with 9 extra lives; instant respawn at death location with full health bar and ammunition restored
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite continues with password system allowing restart from any previously reached stage; score reset to zero on continue (incentivizes full playthrough)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with instant respawn at death point; defeated enemies remain defeated within stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Password system provides agency to practice specific stages; infinite continues remove penalty except score loss, emphasizing experimentation over punitive progression

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary weapon pickups with limited ammunition (more powerful than standard batarangs); melee attacks auto-trigger on close range and deal higher damage
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based linear progression; password system allows skipping to any previously reached stage
replayability_hooks: Score attack incentivized by score reset on continues
Co-op multiplayer mode with dual-character interaction
Multiple difficulty encounters within each stage structure

world_building_techniques: Stage theming around villain personalities (Mr. Freeze's frozen office, Mad Hatter's theater, Scarecrow's graveyard, Harley/Joker's amusement park)
Villain-specific boss encounters tied to narrative plot (Mr. Freeze liberates villains to thwart Batman's interference)
Co-op narrative element: Robin as active partner rather than NPC

boss_design.philosophy: Villain-character boss encounters serve as stage climax; final stage features Mr. Freeze as mastermind; progression through recognizable Batman rogues gallery
notable_bosses: Mr. Freeze (frozen office building final boss)
Harley Quinn and The Joker (amusement park final stage)

faction_indicators: Mr. Freeze's liberated villain alliance (Joker, Two-Face, Mad Hatter, Poison Ivy, Riddler, Clayface)
Batman and Robin as hero faction with co-op coordination
danger_signals: Gotham under freeze threat from Mr. Freeze's scheme
environmental_storytelling: Theater environment reflects Mad Hatter's insanity theme
Frozen office building visualizes Mr. Freeze's power
Graveyard aesthetics tied to Scarecrow's fear domain
Amusement park chaos reflects Harley/Joker's anarchic nature

sources_used: wikipedia: The Adventures of Batman & Robin (video game)
